page: Microsoft Azure D4pls_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine belonging to the DPLSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio, and does not include any local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities for standard compute tasks, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1822.49 and a multi-core score of 7228.15. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for large-block operations, reaching 50.73 GB/sec for read-only and 28.94 GB/sec for write operations, though small-block cached reads are weaker at 282.66 GB/sec. The system demonstrates weak to poor performance in memory-intensive benchmarks, such as the PassMark memory score of 1018.84, and lower-tier results in cryptographic and image processing tests.

This instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for ARM64 development and lightweight scale-out workloads due to its lower memory-to-CPU ratio. It is suited for applications such as static web serving, basic file compression, and lightweight containerized microservices that do not require high memory density or local disk storage.
description: Microsoft Azure D4pls_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine from the DPLSv5 family, powered by a 4-core Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a low-memory profile of 2.0 GB per vCPU, and contains no local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results show average CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 7228.15 and a single-core score of 1822.49, alongside average large-block memory bandwidth. However, memory-intensive tasks and cryptographic operations perform weakly, as reflected in its PassMark memory score of 1018.84. This server is a cost-efficient option for lightweight ARM64 workloads, static web serving, and basic multi-threaded applications that do not demand high memory capacity or local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4pls_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, optimized for cost-efficient, lightweight scale-out workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4pls_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 4 Ampere vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for lightweight compute workloads.
tagline: A cost-efficient ARM64 virtual machine with dedicated compute cores and a low-memory footprint for lightweight scale-out applications.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4-core Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARMv8 processor at 3.0 GHz
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and no local storage
Delivers average CPU performance with a stress-ng multi-core score of 7228.15
Shows weaker performance in memory-intensive and cryptographic workloads
Best suited for static web serving, lightweight ARM64 development, and microservices
